item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that's used in this article is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it's an edited version of this image, which is available under the same license.

item.title: Regarding the recent page revision
item.content:encoded: Just an FYI, I went ahead and changed the audio player from Flash to HTML5, as Flash is rapidly losing support and already can't be viewed by many people, including Firefox and Mobile users. No content changes were made, and the article is otherwise unchanged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really liked it. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this paragraph should be in the Description and not in the Special Containment Procedures, maybe because it is a description of the scip and not a Special Containment Procedure

The first recorded appearance of this phenomenon is dated 19██, filed in Foundation records as an Extranormal Event. However, at least ten repeat-occurrences have since been verified, precipitating numbered status and close observation.

Other than that, it is fairly weird but not over the top, I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. everyone's canon is different, I like to write the Foundations a sprawling organization that can't completely escape bad communication and bureaucracy

2. if the Foundation funnelled funds just to prepare for everything that *COULD* be a major disaster, they'd be completely ineffective

oh, do you just mean the gators? that's just a note along the lines of "possibly necessary to intervene in normal, mundane local government via the usual channels"

and because sometimes the Foundation just watches for a few rounds

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it is unlikely that state budget or resources could adequately contain repeat occurrences.

Why doesn't the Foundation then funnel funds to them so they can be prepared for repeat occurrences?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So…it's a thing we can't explain, can't prevent, and can't really contain, but we can at least contain knowledge of it from reaching the general public, which is really all the Foundation needs to do. For that, I'd say all the prior comments about it not being a scip because we can't contain it (unless of course the subsequent rewrite fixed the issue, which it may well have) aren't really accurate. We can contain it in such a way that we keep civilization from going haywire about it; that is the job of the Foundation at its very core, though of course it would be nice if we could contain the effect instead of just keeping an eye on it. But we are keeping an eye on it, too, so that's good. I'd call it "contained enough".

As for my personal take on the piece itself, I was just going through articles to pass the time, I saw the picture, and I immediately sat up and said "Hello, you look like something interesting!" (Yes, I literally said that out loud.) I didn't realize at first that it was a gator - to me, it looked like some fog clouds, one of which looked sort of like a warped, distended skull shape; in my opinion, the fact that it can be seen that ambiguously prevents it from "looking 'shopped". I have no complaints - if I had to complain, I'd complain about the fact that we really can't tell what it is or what it's doing, but hey, that's the SCP Foundation for you - weird things we can't explain and have no idea how to interpret. What more could a person ask for around here, really? XD

Upvoted happily. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy crap, I used to live in palm beach county!

item.title: Re: Related Question
item.content:encoded: Any excuse to stick Bam Marghera in a steel room and possibly feed him to -682.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can see the alligators worshipping it or something, but making them aggressive doesn't seem to fit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed, and loving it for all these reasons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I strongly disagree. Not every SCP article has to be super scary-dangerous. Alligators being alligators is scary enough without tacking on cheap ante-upping

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is delightfully weird. +1'ed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, I think you nailed it! I particularly like the part about its having been classified previously as an Extranormal Event.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Five minutes from where I live, a woman was out jogging and got pulled off the bike path and killed by an alligator, during the dry season.

ALSO thank you… I will be so pleased if this guy does not dwindle into the negatives. I want to do a related story based off of scary tall-tales my dad used to tell me when I was a kid, about a one-eyed gator.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
yeeeeeey

and well, I wasn't sure which complete rewrite to count »;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Living in an area whete gators can and will do stuff like break into homes, this scares me. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: CD, I asked you to tell me when you posted this so I could upvote.

item.title: REVISION 2
item.content:encoded:
Tried a significant revision, mostly for verbosity and awkward description— but also changed some details and tried to shore up presentation. Kept the core idea, but let me know if it does anything for ya.

Would also appreciate feedback on adding an incident report or field logs, aimed at amping up the atmosphere (not so much the danger, because I am stubborn about that); think it would add, detract, neither…?

item.title: Re: Related Question
item.content:encoded: Probably a good idea!

item.title: Related Question
item.content:encoded: Okay, I thought this image or this one or maybe this one were all way too well-known to use in the entry itself. And I thought using them anyway would be too "*winknudge* IT WAS A SCP ALL ALONG AHAHA" …. which I wanted to avoid. Thoughts?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Welcome. :) That's what feedback is for, after all.

And I didn't mean say "Oh this cloud looks like Alligator mississississisis", I just thought saying "it looks like an alligator" is more lying on your back watching the clouds than filing a scientific report.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, thanks— that's what I was going for. Originally, it only changed water levels, too. I don't want it to really be "concerned" with human fears.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, mainly for the picture. Alot can be done with it.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ack, this is another instances of worrying I was sounding too pedantic— I had exact water levels (I may or may not work with [REDACTED] data on a regular basis), and added "alarming" in a later edit. Might reverse those back, then… Also, I thought it would be silly to give a species to a cloud— because sure, maybe you can tell it is gator and not a croc, but judging species is just a little much?

That last idea is a really intriguing one, though, and much closer to the tone I was going for. I think I was too gentle with the implications, overall, as I wanted it to feel like "this is getting worse and we are probably exacerbating it". If I make any big changes, that will probably be the angle I go for— thanks for the suggestion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. It's intriguing and unexplained without being scary, spooky, or overly mysterious. Props from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I find the delivery a little lacking. What exactly constitute an "abnormal rise in fresh water levels" or "abnormal displacement of wildlife"? "Alarming numbers" of alligators? Also, should probably use the scientific name or say "a species of Alligator" or somesuch when first describing what the cloud looks like.

I do like it more considering it from an "early investigation" angle, but maybe it would do better as a story than an actual SCP. What you've got are notes about a weird thing and what it does, with no ideas on how to contain it, so having it be an article feels… inappropriate? Just a thought.

If anything, you're going for a "why is this happening/what's causing it?" angle, and that may be why people aren't finding it too compelling. So much in the Foundation has no explanation, it's not a big deal. Now if they thought they were on to discovering the triggers and suddenly the next manifestation threw all their data out the window…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks!

And, well, the one point I'm going to be stubborn on is that it not have any fatal or X-but with more danger! effects. Outside of that, I'm not 100% sure what a "hook" would look like—? I'm trying to think how to add something like that without sacrificing the complete ambiguity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. As others mentioned, there's no hook, but I upvote regardless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of a 'ghostly gator god', with some work and a solid hook it could be good. Overall, the writing and tone are okay. Perhaps it could be contained (with effort) if it were not too big. Does it stay in one spot? The photo needs to look more cloudy and less 'shopped.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the overall concept, but there's no hook. However, points for making an SCP that's not necessarily scary, and instead strange and unexplained. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it could make the alligators and other reptiles in the area more aggressive or otherwise change their behavior.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Concept, details, or delivery?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not supposed to be particularly scary— just formless and unreasoning, with no predictable motivations. That said, I waaas a little worried from the get-go that it was toeing the SCP line.

But thanks for the feedback, I'll give thought something more hook-ing that might be added.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I was worried about the photo from the get go, but a footlike cloud might be interesting to play with. I'm hesitant to call it Keter because its effects are manageable and its movement… limited? But maybe the potentially uncontainable state would qualify…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not really sure where are you going with this one. Is it supposed to be scary? We all know fog can be disturbing, but it's been done better. As such, this SCP fails to even get me interested.
That being said it's a solid piece of work, I like the clinical tone. The idea just doesn't really sit with me.

No vote for now, maybe if you found a hook of some sorts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd agree with this if it weren't for that fact that… all SCP reports have to start somewhere, and some take a long time to learn how to contain. I wanted to write a report from that early angle— with surveillance as a viable step toward actual "containment". In that respect, and by hiding its paranormal nature from the locals, they are protecting the human population.

But I do understand the point you're getting at, if nothing else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enh. Doesn't do anything for me. Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd say maybe call it Keter and try to find a different photograph (a different cloud one, it doesn't have to look exactly like an alligator, could just be one shaped like a gator foot and tail or something, and maybe one or two of alligators in weird places?), but I kind of like this one.

item.title: Might do better as a tale
item.content:encoded: It isn't secured or (probably) containable. The Foundation is not protecting the world from it or it from the world. So it fails all three legs of the SCP test.
